title: 10 best herbal creams for back pain

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor, menthol, and thymol, which help reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, providing relief from back pain.

Pain-relieving terpenes: Terpenes like borneol, bornyl acetate, and caryophyllene found in wormwood cream have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing back pain and discomfort.

Antispasmodic flavonoids: Flavonoids like artemisinin and sesquiterpenes in wormwood cream help relax muscle spasms and reduce muscle tension, which can contribute to back pain, providing relief from cramps, stiffness, and strain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds present in wormwood, such as thujone and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and relieve muscle tension. This helps to ease back pain and stiffness, promoting relaxation and flexibility in the affected area. The soothing effects of wormwood creams can also help to calm the nervous system, further reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being. Regular use can lead to improved mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone, a sesquiterpene ketone, has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and pain sensitivity.

Absinthol: Absinthol, a phenolic compound, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help ease back pain by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation in the affected area.

Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate, a sesquiterpene, has been reported to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate back pain by reducing pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Artemisia absinthium contain a compound called absinthin, which is known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: The roots of the plant are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and pain, making them suitable for back pain relief creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 25 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves in 500 milliliters of cold-pressed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter mixture stirring occasionally over low heat for 10 minutes.
Mix the infused oil with the melted wax and butter mixture until well combined and smooth.

remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): CBD has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce back pain by decreasing inflammation and relieving pain sensations.

Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): THC is a natural pain-reliever that binds to cannabinoid receptors in the body, reducing pain perception and promoting relaxation, which can be especially helpful for individuals with chronic back pain.

Terpenes: Terpenes, such as beta-caryophyllene and humulene, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with back pain, while also providing a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa, also known as marijuana, creams helps with back pain because of its unique combination of compounds, including cannabinoids and terpenes. These natural agents work synergistically to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, providing relief from chronic back pain. The topical application of Cannabis sativa creams allows the active ingredients to directly target the affected area, promoting localized relief and reducing the need for systemic medication. This targeted approach can lead to improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes (specifically, myrcene and beta-caryophyllene): These terpenes have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that may help reduce back pain by targeting the underlying inflammation and nerve signals causing discomfort.

Cannabidiol (CBD) and Cannabinoids (specifically, THC): The non-psychoactive compound CBD and the psychoactive compound THC in Cannabis sativa may interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to reduce inflammation, modulate pain perception, and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating back pain.

Beta-Sitosterol: This phytosterol, also known as beta-sitosterol, has anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce muscle spasms, pain, and stiffness associated with back pain by modulating the body's inflammatory response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used to make creams for back pain due to their high content of cannabinoids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds of the Cannabis sativa plant contain high levels of THC and CBD, which are commonly used to create topical creams for pain relief.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain a high concentration of cannabidiol (CBD), which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in creams for back pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of coconut oil with 1 cup of dried cannabis sativa flowers in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids immediately.
Mix 1 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler until melted and smooth.
Combine the infused oil mixture with the melted beeswax mixture and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a bowl.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain by soothing irritated tissues and muscles.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic back pain and stiffness.
Pain-relieving terpenes: Sage contains terpenes like camphor and bornyl acetate, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help relieve back pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The salvinolic acids present in sage have been found to reduce inflammation and ease pain in affected areas, including the back. Additionally, sage creams have antioxidant properties that help to protect the muscles and joints from further damage. As a result, regular application of sage creams can help to alleviate back pain, improve mobility, and promote overall well-being. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective solution for back pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Salvianol A: This terpene has potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and blocking pain signals.
Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce back pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of salvinolic acids and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: Some herbal remedies use stems for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, although their use is less documented.

Roots: The roots might be used in some herbal remedies for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, but their use is less common and less documented compared to leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh salvia officinalis leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested leaves at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture from the leaves.
Steep 1/2 cup of dried leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create the herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the clear liquid extract.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the liquid extract with 8 ounces of a base cream or lotion to create the herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Cayenne pepper: Contains capsaicin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints.
Ginger: Has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which may help alleviate back pain by reducing muscle spasms, stiffness, and swelling.
Willow bark: Contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with back pain because it contains capsaicin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound. When applied topically, capsaicin binds to pain receptors, reducing the transmission of pain signals to the brain. It also increases blood flow and reduces muscle spasms, providing relief from back pain caused by inflammation and strain. Regular use of Capsicum annuum creams may help alleviate chronic back pain and improve overall mobility and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicinoids: Capsaicinoids, particularly capsaicin, help alleviate back pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Capsicum annuum have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with back pain.

Terpenes (specifically, carotenoids and carotenoid-derived compounds): Terpenes, such as capsanthin, in Capsicum annuum help reduce pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of capsaicin, which acts as a pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent.
Seeds: They contain a higher concentration of capsaicinoids, including capsaicin, which is effective in reducing pain and inflammation.
Fruits (specifically, the ripe peppers): They are used as a natural source of capsaicinoids, providing pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties in creams for back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh capsicum annuum fruits and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Infuse the capsicum annuum in 1000ml of carrier oil at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax with the infused oil and heat the mixture at 70-80°c until the wax is fully dissolved.
Add 20g of vitamin e oil and 10g of essential oil to the mixture and stir well before pouring into containers.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to back pain.

Pain-relieving properties: The presence of alkaloids like eupatoriopicrin and parthenolide in the cream have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, providing relief from chronic back pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in the cream help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to muscle and joint degeneration that may exacerbate back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from the plant's roots and flowers contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which work together to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. This natural remedy promotes relaxation and reduces muscle tension, allowing for better mobility and comfort. By applying Echinacea purpurea cream topically, individuals can experience relief from back pain and improve their overall well-being. It's a natural alternative to traditional pain-relieving methods.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain caused by inflammation.

Polyphenols: These antioxidants have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain, and also help reduce oxidative stress that can exacerbate pain.

Cichoric acid: This polyphenol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and improve overall well-being, potentially reducing the severity of back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.

Barks: The barks of Echinacea purpurea contain compounds like isobutylamides that have been reported to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them suitable for back pain relief creams.
Roots: Some sources also suggest that the roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, although the exact mechanism is not fully understood.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 echinacea purpurea roots from a clean and pesticide-free area in late fall or early spring.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 2-3 tablespoons of dried echinacea root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10-15 minutes then let it cool before transferring it to a container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with back pain.
Analgesic: Lavender's analgesic properties can help alleviate pain by blocking the production of pain messengers in the brain, providing relief from chronic back pain.

Muscle relaxant: The sedative and calming effects of lavender oil can help relax tense muscles, reduce muscle spasms, and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The calming effects of the lavender oil can soothe muscle tension and reduce pain in the back. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin allows the active ingredients to target the source of the pain, providing relief from muscle strain and discomfort. Regular application of Lavandula angustifolia cream can lead to improved mobility and reduced back pain over time.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that acts as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has a sedative and calming effect, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and tension that contribute to back pain.
Lavandulol: A terpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, thereby alleviating back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve muscle tension and pain.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants and fragrances that promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, contributing to pain relief.
Seeds: Contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate back pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1/2 cup coconut oil for infusion.
Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in coconut oil for 4-6 hours to create a base mixture.
Combine 1/2 cup beeswax pellets with 1/4 cup shea butter and heat mixture to 120-140 degrees fahrenheit.
Gradually add the infused oil mixture to the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the final mixture into a container and let it cool and set for about 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, alleviating back pain.

Muscle relaxant properties: The herbal extracts present in licorice cream, such as camphor and menthol, have a calming effect on muscle spasms, allowing for increased flexibility and reduced stiffness that can contribute to back pain.

Antioxidant activity: Licorice cream's antioxidant properties help protect the body from free radical damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic low back pain, while also promoting overall spinal health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. As a result, these creams can help alleviate tension and discomfort in the muscles, joints, and ligaments of the back, providing relief from chronic pain and stiffness. Additionally, licorice creams may also help improve blood flow and reduce muscle spasms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and improving joint mobility.
Flavonoids (specifically, Glycyrrhizin): Glycyrrhizin, a flavonoid in Glycyrrhiza glabra, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce pain and stiffness associated with back pain.

Phenolic Acids (specifically, Licorice acid's Phenolic compounds): Phenolic acids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby providing relief from back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate back pain.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which helps to ease back pain and promote overall well-being.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Weigh out 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt for 20 minutes.
Combine 10g of the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder and 5g of vitamin e oil with the melted wax and oil mixture.
Stir the mixture continuously for 5 minutes and pour it into a glass jar to cool for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to set at room temperature for 2 hours before applying it to the skin as needed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The gallic acid present in damask roses helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or muscle strain.
Pain-relieving property: The salicylic acid content in damask roses provides a natural alternative to NSAIDs (nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs), helping to relieve back pain and stiffness.
Antispasmodic property: Damask rose extracts can relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can help alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as sciatica or muscle cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosa damascena, also known as damask rose, creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The creams typically contain essential oils extracted from the Rosa damascena flowers, which have been traditionally used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory compounds in the oil, such as geraniol and linalool, help to soothe and calm the muscles, providing relief from back pain and promoting relaxation. This natural remedy has been shown to be effective in reducing back pain and improving overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Beta-carophyllene: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints, contributing to back pain relief.

Geraniol: A phenolic compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in essential oils and fragrance compounds, which are often used in aromatherapy and skincare products to relieve muscle tension and pain.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful in topical creams for back pain relief.
Roots: They are a source of glycosides and phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of dried rosa damascena flowers in 4 cups of carrier oil at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove flower residue.
Combine 1 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes to melt the beeswax and shea butter.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before whipping it into a creamy consistency using a hand mixer.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Curry leaves contain compounds like curcuminoids and flavonoids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with back pain.

Pain-relieving properties: The herbaceous oil extracted from curry leaves has been shown to possess analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help numb the pain and provide relief from back pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Curry leaves contain compounds that have a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension that often accompany back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Helichrysum italicum, also known as curry plant, creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains sesquiterpene compounds, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the muscles and joints. This natural remedy promotes relaxation and reduces muscle tension, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced discomfort. Additionally, its regenerative properties may aid in the repair of damaged tissue, contributing to long-term relief from back pain. It provides a safe and natural alternative to traditional pain relief methods.
remedy_constituents: Neryl acetate: This terpene helps with back pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area.
Italidione: This ester compound found in Helichrysum italicum has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate back pain.
Phenolic compounds: These compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate back pain.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that contribute to pain relief and muscle relaxation.
Buds: They are a good source of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce back pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of helichrysum italicum flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them in a single layer for 2 weeks.
Infuse 100g of dried helichrysum italicum flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool before whipping it with a hand mixer until light and fluffy.

remedy_properties: Cinchona alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help to reduce pain and swelling associated with back pain.

Tannins: The tannins in Jesuit's Bark cream have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the affected area, thereby alleviating back pain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Jesuit's Bark cream have antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to relief from back pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis, also known as Jesuit's bark, creams helps with back pain because it contains quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces pain and swelling. The cream also contains caffeine, which helps to relax tense muscles and increase blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has analgesic and antispasmodic properties that help to relieve muscle spasms and ease back pain. This natural remedy provides relief without the need for harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_constituents: Quinine alkaloid: Quinine has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and blocking pain signals to the brain.
Quinidine alkaloid: Quinidine has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms that contribute to back pain.
Caffeine: Caffeine has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain, as well as improve blood flow to promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for back pain due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids that help to reduce inflammation and pain.
Barks: Used to make creams for back pain due to their rich content of quinine and other alkaloids that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used to make creams for back pain due to their presence of alkaloids that help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the cinchona officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency and minimal contamination.
Dry the cinchona officinalis leaves and flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried cinchona officinalis leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the cinchona officinalis powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and relax tense muscles. The cream's active ingredient, angelic acid, works to reduce muscle spasms and stiffness, providing rapid relief from chronic back pain. Additionally, the cream's ability to increase blood flow to the affected area promotes healing and reduces swelling, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. As a result, many people have found significant relief from back pain by using angelica creams as part of their treatment regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Angelica creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as angelic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, providing relief from back pain.
Muscle relaxants: Angelica contains muscle-relaxing compounds like estragole, which can help to relax tense muscles and reduce spasms, easing discomfort and stiffness associated with back pain.

Analgesic properties: Angelica creams also contain analgesic compounds like isoalantolactone, which helps to numb the affected area, reducing pain perception and providing relief from acute or chronic back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are used to create creams that target inflammation and reduce pain associated with back issues.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome's high concentration of angelic acid and isoangelic acid makes it an effective ingredient in creams for relieving muscle spasms and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried angelica archangelica root and stem, carefully selecting fresh and organic material.
Combine the harvested herb with 30 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar, stirring until well combined.
Place the mixture in a warm oven at 40 degrees celsius for 12 hours to infuse, allowing the herbal compounds to extract.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of the herbal infused oil in a double boiler, stirring until melted and well combined to create the final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with back pain because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic that targets sore muscles and joints. When applied topically to affected areas, the cream reduces inflammation and relieves tension, providing fast-acting relief from acute or chronic back pain. The warm, soothing sensation also helps to relax the muscles, reducing stiffness and improving range of motion. As the cream absorbs into the skin, it continues to provide a gentle, yet effective, relief from discomfort, making wintergreen creams an excellent natural remedy for back pain sufferers.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of wintergreen oil, helps to relieve muscle and joint pain by numbing the skin and reducing inflammation, providing rapid relief from back pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wintergreen oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as methyl salicylate, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to back pain caused by conditions like arthritis or sprains.

Analgesic effects: The analgesic properties of wintergreen oil, including its ability to block pain pathways and reduce pain transmission, help provide effective pain relief for back pain sufferers without causing addiction or other side effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing high levels of salicylic acid, the roots are used to create a natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving agent in creams for back pain.
Rhizomes: As the thickened underground stems of the plant, rhizomes provide a concentrated source of wintergreen's medicinal compounds, making them ideal for cream production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the early morning after dew has dried off completely.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place with good air circulation at room temperature for 7 days.
Steep 10 grams of dried gaultheria procumbens in 500 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 2 hours under gentle heat.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and setting aside for later use.
Mix 30 percent of the infused gaultheria procumbens oil with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler at low heat until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with back pain because of its unique anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The gel-like substance present in aloe vera has been shown to reduce swelling and relieve discomfort associated with back pain. Additionally, its cooling and soothing effects help to relax tense muscles and calm irritated tissues, providing quick relief from acute and chronic back pain episodes. This natural remedy is particularly effective for alleviating lower back pain, sciatica, and strains, making it an excellent alternative to traditional pain management methods.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe creams contain compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with back pain.
Analgesic properties: Aloe vera's analgesic properties help to numb the area affected by back pain, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Wound healing and tissue regeneration properties: The antioxidants and vitamins present in aloe vera creams promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can be beneficial for treating chronic back pain conditions that involve skin irritation or wounds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside the leaves is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it an effective ingredient for soothing and reducing back pain.
Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain anthraquinones, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves using scissors or a sharp knife.
Extract 3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and mix it with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Add 1 teaspoon of turmeric powder and 1/2 teaspoon of ginger powder to the mixture.
Stir in 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 5 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized glass jar, store it at room temperature for 24 hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with back pain because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that effectively soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints. The cream's active ingredients, such as calendula extract, penetrate deep into the skin to reduce swelling and ease stiffness, providing quick relief from acute back pain. Additionally, pot marigold's natural antioxidant properties help to promote healing and reduce scar tissue formation, leading to long-term pain reduction and improved flexibility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with back pain, allowing for improved mobility and reduced pain.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in pot marigold cream work to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage, promoting healing and reducing chronic pain.
Analgesic: Pot marigold cream's analgesic properties help numb the painful area, providing relief from acute back pain and discomfort, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and relieve back pain by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds have analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, making them an effective ingredient in topical creams for back pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried calendula flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the ground calendula powder with 50ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine, then heat the mixture at 60°c for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth consistency within 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months, allowing the ingredients to mature for optimal efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red shiso creams helps with back pain because of its unique blend of herbs, which work together to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate pressure on the spine and surrounding tissues, while its analgesic components numb the pain, providing instant relief. Additionally, red shiso's natural moisturizing effects soothe dry skin and promote healthy joint function, further contributing to a reduction in back discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Red shiso's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as shisonenol, help reduce swelling and relieve pain associated with back pain by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Muscle relaxant: The camphor content in red shiso has a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and tension that often contribute to back pain.
Pain-relieving: Red shiso's analgesic properties, due to the presence of cinnamic acid, help block pain signals from reaching the brain, providing natural relief for back pain sufferers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints, reducing discomfort caused by back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lithospermum erythrorhizon roots in late summer weighing approximately 250 grams.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 55°c.
Grind dried lithospermum erythrorhizon root powder to fine consistency using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of ground lithospermum erythrorhizon powder with 80 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 60°c while stirring occasionally to create a smooth and creamy consistency suitable for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with back pain because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce muscle soreness and stiffness. The warming properties of ginger also help to relax tight muscles, reducing tension and discomfort in the back. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of creams can help to soothe dry skin and reduce irritation, allowing for improved circulation and reduced pain. This combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing properties makes herbal ginger creams a natural and effective remedy for back pain relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, alleviating back pain caused by arthritis, muscle strains, or other inflammatory conditions.

Analgesic and anesthetic properties: The natural oils and extracts present in herbal ginger creams have analgesic (pain-relieving) and anesthetic properties, helping to numb the affected area, reducing discomfort, and providing relief from chronic back pain.

Muscle relaxant compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain muscle relaxant compounds like camphor and menthol, which help soothe tense muscles, reduce spasms, and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating back pain caused by muscle strain or tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in natural compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Rhizomes: The main storage organ of the ginger plant, rhizomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, making them effective in relieving back pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh turmeric rhizomes and dried ginger root and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Melt one cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until clear.
Combine the ground turmeric and ginger powder with three tablespoons of beeswax pellets in a small bowl and mix well for 5 minutes.
Gradually add the turmeric-ginger mixture to the melted coconut oil whisking continuously for 2 minutes until smooth.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least two hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saffron creams helps with back pain because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that target the affected areas, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness. The natural compounds in saffron cream work to reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Additionally, saffron's potent antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, promoting a healthy environment for optimal healing and recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Saffron's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as crocin and crocetin, help reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to back pain.
Pain-relieving properties: Saffron contains analgesic compounds that can help relieve back pain by reducing nerve impulses and blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.

Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids and other bioactive compounds in saffron creams may help relax tense muscles, reduce muscle spasms, and improve flexibility, all of which can alleviate back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain.
Barks: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and relax muscles affected by back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried crocus sativus flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered crocus sativus with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the oil extract.
Combine the oil extract with 50 grams of beeswax and heat it gently in a double boiler until the wax has fully melted and incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes before filling sterilized tubes or jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree peony creams helps with back pain because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work in harmony to reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle tension. The anti-inflammatory properties of tree peony help to soothe sore muscles and joints, while the soothing agents calm irritated nerves and promote relaxation. This natural approach can provide long-lasting relief from chronic back pain, allowing individuals to move with greater ease and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal tree peony creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain by decreasing swelling and discomfort.

Analgesic and anesthetic effects: The creams' analgesic properties help numb the affected area, reducing pain perception, while their anesthetic effects can block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing fast relief from back pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Herbal tree peony creams contain compounds that relax muscles, which helps reduce muscle spasms and strain in the back, leading to improved mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds, tree peony roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate back pain.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, rhyzomes provide potent analgesic effects in treating back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest paeonia suffruticosa roots in the fall when mature, dry them thoroughly and grind into fine powder.
Combine 250 grams of ground paeonia suffruticosa roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Infuse the mixture for at least 4 hours or overnight by placing it in a double boiler or a dark glass jar and letting it sit at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, squeezing out as much oil as possible from the solids.

Mix the resulting herbal oil with 250 grams of beeswax, 100 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 50 milliliters of distilled water to create the finished cream, allowing it to solidify at room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with back pain because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and relax tense muscles, reducing discomfort and stiffness. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and taraxadiol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, dandelion creams can help to improve blood flow and reduce muscle spasms, making them a natural and effective solution for alleviating back pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the back.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which is often associated with chronic back pain conditions like arthritis or fibromyalgia.
Natural analgesic: Dandelion contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has natural analgesic and anti-pain properties that can help soothe back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Fruits: Containing flavonoids, fatty acids, and terpenes, the fruits of dandelion plant help to relax muscles, reduce spasms, and ease back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in bulk quantities of 100g.
Combine the harvested ingredients with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for a ratio of 20%.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c to 70°c for a period of 1 hour to ensure proper infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10g of beeswax and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and seal tightly after preparation time has elapsed.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for back pain are topical creams infused with natural herbs that help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving blood flow. The best herbal creams for back pain include Arnica montana, Curcuma longa, Capsicum annuum, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Salix alba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Mentha x piperita, Cannabis sativa, and Melaleuca alternifolia. If left untreated, back pain can significantly impact daily life, limiting mobility, causing sleep disturbances, and hindering productivity, ultimately affecting mental and physical well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to back pain that are treated with herbal creams are various musculoskeletal issues, such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and sciatica. Herbal creams containing ingredients like capsaicin, arnica, and menthol can provide relief from joint and muscle pain, reducing inflammation and discomfort. These creams can also help alleviate symptoms of conditions like tendonitis, bursitis, and strain injuries, making them a natural alternative to conventional treatments for many painful conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with back pain is a blend of arnica, peppermint, and ginger creams. Arnica cream reduces inflammation and soothes muscle tension, while peppermint cream cools and relaxes the area, alleviating spasms. Ginger cream, with its anti-inflammatory properties, further reduces pain and promotes healing. Applying a combination of these creams to the affected area can provide effective relief from back pain, allowing for a more comfortable and pain-free daily life. Regular use can also promote long-term muscle recovery.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Spine, herbal creams may be beneficial for back pain due to the effectiveness of certain herbal medicines in reducing pain. The study included ten trials, with two high-quality trials utilizing Harpagophytum procumbens (Devil's claw) finding strong evidence for short-term improvements in pain and rescue medication for daily doses standardized to 50 mg or 100 mg harpagoside. Additionally, two moderate-quality trials utilizing Salix alba (White willow bark) found moderate evidence for short-term improvements in pain and rescue medication for daily doses standardized to 120 mg or 240 mg salicin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with back pain because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from muscle tension and soreness. Additionally, Arnica montana creams promote blood circulation, which helps to nourish and repair damaged tissues, leading to faster recovery and reduced pain. This natural remedy is a popular alternative to traditional pain relief methods.
remedy_constituents: Thiophene derivatives: They have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area of the back.
Flavonoids: They possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the muscles and tissues of the back.
Helenalin: It is a sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the back muscles and joints.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Arnica montana are the most commonly used part to make creams for back pain because they contain high concentrations of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Arnica montana are also used to make creams for back pain because they possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation.
Buds: The buds of Arnica montana are used to make creams for back pain because they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried arnica montana flowers in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and cocoa butter to create a creamy base.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax and cocoa butter melt and blend smoothly.
Pour the hot mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with back pain because they contain a high concentration of curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound. Curcumin reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are major contributors to back pain. By applying curcuma longa cream directly to the affected area, the curcumin is absorbed into the skin and muscles, providing localized relief from pain and stiffness. Regular use of curcuma longa creams can also promote healing and reduce muscle spasms, leading to long-term relief from back pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, leading to relief from back pain.
Gingerol: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Volatile oils (including Zingiberene and Turmerone): They have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from back pain by reducing inflammation and pain-causing chemicals.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa's rhyzomes are primarily used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used as a rich source of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain.
Barks: Curcuma longa's barks are used to extract bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to the relief of back pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100 grams of curcuma longa root powder and 50 grams of beeswax.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 2 minutes.
Combine the curcuma longa powder with 10 grams of sweet almond oil and 5 grams of coconut oil.
Add the melted beeswax mixture to the curcuma longa and oil mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams helps with back pain because they contain capsaicin, a natural compound that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. By reducing the pain signal transmission, capsaicin creams can help alleviate back pain caused by inflammation, muscle strain, or nerve damage. The heat produced by capsaicin also increases blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and relaxation of tense muscles, providing relief from back pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: It helps reduce back pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the back muscles, which can contribute to pain.
Volatile oils: They contain compounds that can help relax muscles and improve blood flow, reducing muscle tension and pain in the back.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Capsaicin present in the fruits is the primary active compound used to relieve pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals.
Seeds: Like the fruits, the seeds of Capsicum annuum also contain capsaicin, which is used to reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are another source of capsaicin, which is often extracted and used in creams to provide pain relief for back pain sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 fresh capsicum annuum fruits with bright red color when fully ripe and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the harvested capsicum annuum fruits at 40-50°c for 3-5 hours in a food dehydrator or oven.
Grind 50g of dried capsicum annuum fruits into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground capsicum annuum powder with 100g of shea butter, 50g of coconut oil, and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency and allows it to cool before packaging.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease muscle tension. The active compound, gingerol, has natural analgesic and antispasmodic effects that relieve pain and stiffness in the back. This natural remedy promotes relaxation and improves blood circulation, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area. Regular application of Zingiber officinale creams can provide long-lasting relief from back pain, promoting overall well-being and a reduction in discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: They help with back pain by reducing inflammation and pain through their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Shogaols: They help with back pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body and reducing inflammation.
Zingiberene: It helps with back pain by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation through its immunomodulatory effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: Although the terms 'roots' and 'rhizomes' are often used interchangeably, the roots of the Zingiber officinale plant are not as commonly used as the rhizomes, but still contain some of the same pain-relieving compounds.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale contain a higher concentration of certain compounds like gingerol than the mature rhizomes, making them a popular choice for topical creams.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of zingiber officinale powder with 1 tablespoon of distilled water in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes while stirring constantly to create a thick paste.
Mix in 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax to create a smooth consistency.
Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of shea butter to enhance moisturizing properties.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 10 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with back pain because they contain eucalyptol, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and relaxes tense muscles. The cooling effect of the cream helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area, providing quick relief. Additionally, eucalyptus oil has a decongestant property that helps to open up airways and improve circulation, which can aid in reducing muscle strain and promoting healing, ultimately alleviating back pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It reduces inflammation and pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemical messengers that promote pain and inflammation.
α-Pinene: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relax muscles and reduce pain and stiffness in the back.
Limonene: It has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to relax muscle spasms and reduce pain and inflammation in the back.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in eucalyptol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound, which helps alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Contains salicylic acid, a natural pain-reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to soothe and reduce pain in the muscles.

Essential oil from Leaves: The oil extracted from the leaves of Eucalyptus globulus is widely used in creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve back pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200ml of eucalyptus globulus essential oil from 1kg of fresh leaves using steam distillation for 2 hours.
Mix 1kg of shea butter with 500ml of beeswax and 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine 50ml of eucalyptus globulus essential oil with 250ml of distilled water and 10g of vitamin e oil in a bowl.
Gradually add the eucalyptus globulus mixture to the shea butter mixture and heat for 10 minutes at 60°c.
Pour the final mixture into 100g containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salix alba creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The extract from the willow tree, which is the primary component of the cream, contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. When applied topically, salicin helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected area, providing relief from back pain. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming properties can help to relax muscles and promote a sense of well-being, further contributing to its pain-relieving effects.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Acts as a pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with back pain.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate pain and discomfort in the back.
Tannins: Display anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax muscles, reduce muscle spasms, and alleviate pain in the back.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Salix alba are used to make creams for back pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Salix alba are used to make creams for back pain because they contain salicin, a compound that reduces pain and inflammation.
Buds: The buds of Salix alba are used to make creams for back pain due to their high content of salicin, which provides relief from pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried salix alba bark from a reputable source and store it in a cool dry place.
Weigh and mix 50g of the dried bark with 200ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours then strain it through a cheesecloth into a separate container.
Combine 100ml of the infused oil with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before transferring it to a tub.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with back pain because they contain the anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root. The compounds present in the root, such as glycyrrhizin, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the muscles and joints. By relaxing tense muscles and reducing pain, these creams can provide relief from back pain, allowing individuals to move more freely and engage in activities without discomfort. This natural remedy offers a non-invasive approach to managing back pain and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with back pain, making it easier to manage and alleviate symptoms.

Flavonoids: As potent antioxidants, flavonoids help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to pain relief and improved muscle function, making it easier to manage back pain.

Glycyrrhizic acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the back muscles and joints, making it easier to manage back pain and improve overall mobility.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are primarily used to make creams for back pain due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in creams for back pain because they contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in creams for back pain due to their content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a 500ml water bath at 100°c for 1 hour.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and collect the resulting herbal infusion.
Combine 100g of candelilla wax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Gradually add 50g of the herbal infusion to the candelilla wax and shea butter mixture.
Pour the resulting mixture into a mold and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The menthol in peppermint oil penetrates deep into the muscles, reducing tension and relieving discomfort. The cream's cooling sensation also helps to relax the muscles, reducing stiffness and pain. Additionally, mentha x piperita's ability to improve circulation and reduce spasms contributes to its effectiveness in managing back pain. Regular application can help to alleviate chronic pain and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps relieve back pain by creating a cooling sensation that numbs the affected area, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Menthone: It acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and swelling associated with back pain.
Caryophyllene: It has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and pain in the back by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems contain menthol, which helps to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the muscles.
Roots: Roots are a source of menthol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them useful for treating back pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 200g of fresh menth x piperita leaves in 1l of water at 85°c for 20 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 100g of cetearyl alcohol, 20g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the cooled infusion to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a mold and refrigerate for 30 minutes to set completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa creams helps with back pain because they contain cannabinoids, which interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The creams work by releasing a localized dose of cannabinoids directly to the affected area, allowing for targeted relief without the systemic effects of ingested cannabis. This targeted approach can provide effective relief from chronic back pain, improving mobility and reducing discomfort, allowing individuals to resume their normal activities with greater ease and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Cannabidiol (CBD): It helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system, which can decrease the perception of pain.
Beta-caryophyllene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with back pain.
Myrcene: It acts as an analgesic, reducing the transmission of pain signals to the brain and providing relief from back pain.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are rich in cannabinoids, particularly THC and CBD, which are potent pain relievers and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Cannabis sativa contain a high concentration of CBD and other cannabinoids, making them a popular choice for creams and topicals to alleviate back pain.
Seeds: The seeds are a rich source of CBD and other beneficial compounds, and are often used to create CBD-infused creams and oils for pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 4 cups of cannabis sativa flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut oil for 6 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean bowl to separate the plant material from the oil.
Combine 1 cup of beeswax with 1 cup of shea butter and 1/2 cup of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Gradually add 1 cup of the cannabis sativa oil infusion to the melted wax and butter mixture while stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into a mold and allow it to cool and harden for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with back pain because it contains tea tree oil, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and eases discomfort in the muscles. The cream's analgesic properties also block pain signals to the brain, providing quick relief from aching and stiffness. Additionally, tea tree oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection in open wounds, reducing the risk of further injury and promoting faster healing of back pain. Regular application can lead to improved mobility and reduced back pain.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These phenolic compounds help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and relieving oxidative stress in the affected areas.
Cineol (Eucalyptol): Cineol has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help ease back pain by numbing the pain and reducing swelling in the muscles and joints.
Cymene: This monoterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that contribute to its pain-relieving properties, which are beneficial for back pain relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate back pain.
Stems: Stems are used to obtain a compound called cineole, which has been shown to have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce back pain.
Barks: Barks are utilized for their content of terpinen-4-ol, a compound that has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating back pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml of distilled water in a saucepan over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Combine 20g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with the infused water let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean bowl discard the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat until melted.
Combine the infused liquid with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and whip until smooth.
